---
title: "Having problems installing ubuntu"
date: 2008-10-28
forum: General Help
---

### Post by Jameshardy88 on 2008-10-28
Hi,

i have done the first part of the install and dutifully restated my system as prompted. upon restart i selected ubuntu and it seems to begin the installation (i get the ubuntu logo come up). after that i get this message...

"(initramfs) [    51.968563] sd 4:0:0:0 [sdb] assuming drive chache :write through
[    51.974953] sd 4:0:0:0 [sdb] assuming drive chache :write through

after which i get the flashing "_" and nothing seems to happen

i assume i am supposed to enter somthing here i am just unsure what, as i was told that i would not need to do anything except let it run on its own

thanks for any help, James

Edit: just incase it is relevent i installed Wubi on my external hard drive

---

### Post by ago on 2008-10-28
Press esc at boot after "ubuntu" and try the other options, in particular "safe graphic mode">

---

### Post by Jameshardy88 on 2008-10-28
> **ago said:**
> Press esc at boot after "ubuntu" and try the other options, in particular "safe graphic mode">

Thanks for the suggestion, i just tried that and this time it began to install but  stopped whilst appearing to be doing somthing to my external hard drive

---

### Post by ago on 2008-10-28
It's possible that earlier you interrupted an installation (although in black screen). Uninstall, reinstall and go for safe graphic mode.

---

### Post by Jameshardy88 on 2008-10-28
do i need to completely uninstall wubi and re-download or just re-set my pc and restart the installation]

Edit: nevermind i have found the answer to my own question

---

### Post by ago on 2008-10-28
That is not an error message, does it work with acpi=off?
